title: EVgo's Q3 Earnings Call: Our Top 5 Analyst Questions
text: EVgo delivered strong third-quarter results, with revenue of $92.3 million exceeding analyst expectations by 0.7%. The electric vehicle charging network operator demonstrated improvement in adjusted EBITDA and a very strong liquidity position, according to CEO Badar Khan. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of -$0.02, significantly outperforming the -$0.16 estimate. Revenue grew 36.7% year-over-year. Operating margins improved substantially to -36.9% from -47.1% in the prior year. Management raised full-year revenue guidance to $377.5 million and EBITDA guidance to $4 million. Five key analyst inquiries emerged: EV demand relationship—RBC Capital Markets questioned long-term growth drivers, with management emphasizing returns on capital over short-term EV forecasts. Deployment delays—JPMorgan asked about stall deployment pace, with Khan noting a strategic shift toward eXtend charging stalls. Seasonality and profitability—Stifel inquired about EBITDA sustainability. NACS adoption—ROTH Capital Partners discussed connector utilization and dynamic pricing strategies. Growth targets—Morningstar questioned whether an autonomous vehicle contract closeout affected long-term expansion plans, with management confirming unchanged growth targets.
